nodes.name: agents.md
nodes.summary: Language-agnostic rule document defining agent orchestration strategy for the ECC harness, covering available agents, when to invoke them immediately, parallel task execution patterns, and multi-perspective analysis.

nodes.name: code-review.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive code review standards rule covering review triggers, checklists, security review triggers, severity levels, agent-assisted review workflow, and common issues to catch across security, quality, and performance categories.

nodes.name: coding-style.md
nodes.summary: Language-agnostic coding style rules enforcing immutability as a critical principle, KISS/DRY/YAGNI principles, file organization, error handling, input validation, naming conventions, and a code quality checklist for code smells.

nodes.name: development-workflow.md
nodes.summary: Rule document describing the standard feature implementation workflow, providing a step-by-step process from task intake through implementation to delivery for ECC-managed projects.

nodes.name: git-workflow.md
nodes.summary: Rule document specifying commit message format conventions and pull request workflow requirements used across all ECC-managed language environments.

nodes.name: hooks.md
nodes.summary: Rule document describing the ECC hooks system, covering available hook types, auto-accept permission policies, and TodoWrite best practices for maintaining structured task lists.

nodes.name: patterns.md
nodes.summary: Rule document defining common design patterns recommended across all ECC-managed projects, including skeleton project setup conventions, the repository pattern, and standardized API response formatting.

nodes.name: performance.md
nodes.summary: Rule document covering performance optimization strategies including model selection guidance, context window management techniques, extended thinking and plan mode usage, and build troubleshooting procedures.

nodes.name: security.md
nodes.summary: Rule document specifying mandatory security checks, secret management practices, and security response protocols that must be applied across all ECC-managed codebases.

nodes.name: testing.md
nodes.summary: Rule document mandating 80% minimum test coverage, test-driven development practices, failure troubleshooting steps, agent-assisted testing support, and the AAA (Arrange-Act-Assert) test structure pattern with naming conventions.
